column5: BSc Mathematics with a Year in Industry

column2: Thought about average random number (if normal distribution) and what others would likely pick.
column3: I chose my decision number of 30 because I wanted to go roughly halfway between zero and the halfway mark. In hindsight, I wish I went closer to the halfway mark now!
column4: I selected 71 at the begining as I felt it was an appropriate risk to get into the second round and if I had gotten a random number that had lowered my score significantly, I would walk away with at least more than half of the possible money I could get if I didnt get to the second round. I got into the second round and chose 63 as my decision number because I wanted to make at least 8. I was unlucky in this instance and got a random number of -60 so, I lost but got an amount that I would be happy with.
column5: I decided that picking a higher number was worth the higher cost as i was more likely to make it through both stages
column6: Decision making process - The benefit of making it past the first round seemed greater than losing within the first round and so i was inclined to go for a higher number, but wanted to balance this by not going too high that it would adversely affect my costs\r\nFor round 2 I didn't feel as strongly about passig onto the next round and so i went with a more consevative decision number in the hope that my opponent would go lower than me, although i was also majorly affected by the random number in this case
column7: For first round, I was slightly more conservative, when I reached the second round, I roughly doubled my decision number.
column8: I wanted a number that was not too high because of the costs and random number.  At the same time, I did not want a number too low as I wanted to be in the second around.
column9: I did not want to risk receiving less than the 5.73, so chose 3 as it was higher than 0 but still did not have any associated costs
column10: When halving the decision number I rounded up to get 63. A t this point the cost is increasing by 0.06 for every 1 for the decision number. I therefore went down the table until I found the last increase of 0.06, which occurred up to 78. Thus I picked 78
column11: For the first  stage I just wanted to make sure that I would be able to get onto the second stage as I thought pretty much no matter what if I can get onto the second stage I will earn more than if I only complete the first stage. In the second stage I saw how much I had spent on the first stage so I picked a smaller number.
column12: I came to conclussion that numbers areound 70ish would be the highest.
column13: I randomly decide on the decision number with the lowest possible cost since each of the players in this experiment wanted to maximise their profits or revenue.
